validation.passwordRequired: Enter a password with at least 6 characters.
validation.teamNameRequired: Enter a team name with at least 2 characters.
validation.uniqueMembersRequired: Each team member name must be unique.
validation.registrationFailed: Registration failed. Check the email, password, and Firebase connection.
validation.signInFailed: Sign in failed. Check the email, password, and Firebase connection.

onboarding.subtitle: Initialize Your Journey
onboarding.signInProfile: Sign in to continue your team's work

parachute.title: Parachute Drop
parachute.objective: Design, build, and test a parachute for a small toy so it lands more slowly and safely.
equipment: Mobile phone with STEMM Lab app
Small toy, such as a toy soldier
instructions: Drop the toy without a parachute and record the fall as your baseline test.
Build a parachute using the provided materials.
Drop the toy from the same height and record the fall.
Review speed and landing accuracy results in the app.
Redesign and test up to three prototypes within 20 minutes.
Upload videos, results, and team reflections.
parachute.discussion: Gravity pulls the toy downward. The parachute adds drag, which pushes upward and slows the fall.
parachute.timerInstruction: Drop the toy from the same height each time. Start the timer as it leaves your hand and stop when it first touches the ground.
parachute.measurementHint: Enter the height and the time your team measured. Results are calculated from your observation.
parachute.enterMeasurement: Enter a valid height and time first.
parachute.videoInstruction: Use slow-motion video to mark the first impact point and check whether the toy lands inside the target zone.
parachute.uploadVideoSub: Choose a video from this device, then drag the timeline to replay or fast forward.
parachute.rankedBy: Ranked by slowest and safest landing
writeUpFields: Predict which parachute design was the best.
Record the times for each design.
Were you correct about the timings?
What design was the easiest to make?

sound.title: Sound Pollution Hunter
sound.overview: Students measure and compare sound levels in different classroom activities.
equipment: Mobile phone with STEMM Lab app
instructions: Measure noise from different actions, such as dropping pens or books, talking, walking, and stamping your feet.
Record sound levels and locations.
Map loud and quiet zones.
sound.actionLoggerSub: Select an action to start a 5-second sample
sound.actionReportSub: Review the action records your team logged from the live meter.
sound.noActionLogs: No action records yet. Go back to the live meter, name an action, and add a record.
sound.samplesNote: Samples are recorded for 5 seconds. Tap any action to begin recording.
sound.writeSub: Compare your predictions with actual results
writeUpFields: Predict which action created the loudest sound.
Should we wear ear muffs in your classroom?

handFan.title: Hand Fan Challenge
handFan.overview: Students test how air movement affects flexible materials.
instructions: Stand paper upright on a table.
Fan air from 30 cm away.
Observe and record movement.
Repeat with different fan designs and fan distances of 15 cm, 30 cm, and 45 cm.
Repeat with cardboard instead of vertical paper.
handFan.angleSub: Adjust the angle to match the paper bend.
handFan.photoSub: Capture the bend with degree overlay
handFan.cameraPermission: Camera permission is needed for photo proof.
handFan.noLeaderboardData: Enter matrix measurements to create your team leaderboard entry.
handFan.topPerformers: Top performers in the Hand Fan Challenge
writeUpFields: Predict which fan design makes the paper move the most.
How does material stiffness affect the bend angle?
How does distance from the fan affect bending?

earthquake.title: Earthquake Structure
earthquake.overview: Students design structures that withstand vibration, simulating earthquakes.
instructions: Build an anti-vibration layer by folding paper or cardboard.
Place a flat cardboard platform on top.
Place the phone in the centre and activate vibration mode in the STEMM App.
Modify the structure to reduce movement, such as adding more pillars or folds.
earthquake.rankedBy: Ranked by lowest vibration and highest stability
writeUpFields: Predict which fold design makes the phone move the least.

reaction.title: Reaction Board
reaction.overview: Students measure reaction time, coordination, and improvement through repeated digital and physical challenges.
instructions: Tap the screen as soon as the hidden button appears.
Record reaction time and rotate through each team member.
Repeat using the non-dominant hand.
Trace a moving shape on the screen.
Review accuracy and delay.
reaction.tapWhenBlue: Tap when the button turns blue
reaction.tracingSub: Follow the curved line with your finger
reaction.globalRankSub: Your team vs. other grade levels

breathing.title: Breathing Pace
breathing.overview: Students analyse breathing patterns at rest and after exercise.
instructions: Place the phone gently on the chest.
Record breathing at rest.
Perform light exercise.
Jog one minute on the spot or complete star jumps.
Record breathing again and compare results.
Rotate for each team member.
breathing.placementSub: Position your phone correctly for accurate tracking

humanPerformance.title: Human Performance
humanPerformance.overview: Measure speed, smoothness, and control during safe stretching movements.
equipment: Mobile phone with STEMM Lab app
A team partner to observe and record results
instructions: Hold the phone firmly in one hand and activate the vibration sensor.
Perform the guided movement slowly as shown in the app.
Record the vibration while you move.
Repeat the activity with vibration feedback enabled.
Review speed, smoothness, and range-of-motion data.
Upload results and reflect as a group.
humanPerformance.tip: Move slowly, keep clear space around you, and stop if the stretch feels uncomfortable.
humanPerformance.sensorInstruction: Vibration amplitude is mapped to body movement.
writeUpFields: Which movement was the hardest to keep the vibration low?
